name: BoltzGen
description: Designs nanobodies, antibodies, proteins, peptides, and cyclotides to bind protein targets. Designs proteins to bind small molecules. Specifies binding sites or exclusion zones on targets. Supports constraints: disulfide bonds, secondary structure (helix/loop/sheet), cyclic peptides. 67% experimental success rate: 6 of 9 targets yielded nanomolar nanobody binders with no prior structural data.
capabilities: Designs nanobodies, antibodies, proteins, peptides, and cyclotides to bind protein targets
Designs proteins to bind small molecules
Specifies binding sites or exclusion zones on targets
Supports constraints: disulfide bonds, secondary structure (helix/loop/sheet), cyclic peptides
67% experimental success rate: 6 of 9 targets yielded nanomolar nanobody binders with no prior structural data

pdbFile.description: Target protein structure in PDB format (required for protein target tasks)

targetChains.description: Chain IDs of the target. Defaults to all chains.

bindingSiteResidues.description: Specific residues on the target to bind

lengthRange.description: Length range for the designed protein/peptide

scaffoldType.description: Use default (de novo) or custom framework scaffold

frameworkPdbFile.description: Custom framework structure for scaffold-based design

frameworkSequence.description: Framework sequence with CDR placeholders using ranges for nanobody/antibody design

cdrRegions.description: CDR regions to design in the framework

cyclic.description: Whether to design cyclic peptides (peptide task only)

targetSmiles.description: SMILES string for small molecule target (small_molecule_binder task only)

omitAminoAcids.description: Amino acids to exclude from the design

batchSize.description: Designs per job when auto-batching is enabled

budget.description: Final number of designs optimized for diversity and quality
